data.description:
## Why
Only Claude Code (remote) and OpenCode actually expose a readable session env var; Codex
explicitly does NOT (no supported programmatic access), and Cursor has none yet. So beyond
Claude Code, the portable `REBAR_SESSION_ID` must be populated by each harness's own
lifecycle mechanism, or the feature no-ops there.

## What
Ship copy-paste shims:
- A Codex `SessionStart` hook (Codex has its own hooks) that exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`
  (derived from the `~/.codex/sessions/` rollout id or a generated id).
- A Cursor `.cursor/environment.json` start-script snippet that exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`
  for cloud agents (and a local shell/tmux note).
- Per-harness install docs.

## Scope
- IN: the Codex + Cursor shims and their docs.
- OUT: Gemini CLI / other harnesses (follow-up); the core record/read (S1-S3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Codex SessionStart hook exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`; a Codex claim records
      `claimed_session`.
- [ ] Cursor start-script snippet exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`; a Cursor-agent claim records it.
- [ ] Per-harness install docs shipped.

## Depends on
- S5 (the resolver recognizes the harness vars + records the harness tag).
data.title: Per-harness capture shims: Codex SessionStart hook + Cursor start-script snippet
